extra.message: User-controlled input 'search_term' from request.json['search'] is interpolated directly into a SQL query string using Python % string formatting with no sanitization or parameterization. The raw string is then passed to db.engine.execute(), allowing arbitrary SQL injection.

The 404 error handler embeds request.url directly into a template string via Python % formatting before calling render_template_string(). An attacker can inject Jinja2 template expressions (e.g. {{config}}) in the URL path, which will be executed server-side. The template context also includes 'locals', exposing all local variables. This is Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI).

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine

extra.message: The /search error handler embeds the exception message str(e) directly into a template string via Python % formatting before calling render_template_string(). Since the exception originates from a SQL-injection-vulnerable query, the error message contains attacker-controlled input, enabling Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). The template context also includes 'locals', exposing all local variables.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine

extra.message: The insecure_verify() function calls jwt.decode(token, verify=False), which completely disables JWT signature verification. This function is used by the /get/<cust_id> endpoint (line 227), allowing an attacker to forge arbitrary JWT tokens and bypass authentication entirely.

The /yaml_hammer endpoint uses yaml.load() (without specifying a safe loader) to deserialize user-uploaded YAML files. With PyYAML==3.12, the default yaml.load() can instantiate arbitrary Python objects, leading to remote code execution. yaml.safe_load() should be used instead.

Multiple cryptographic secrets are hardcoded in the application source: SECRET_KEY_HMAC='secret' (line 26), SECRET_KEY_HMAC_2='am0r3C0mpl3xK3y' (line 27), and Flask secret_key (line 28). These keys are used for JWT signing/verification and session management. Anyone with source code access can forge valid JWTs and session cookies.

User passwords are hashed using MD5 (hashlib.md5(password).hexdigest()) without any salt. MD5 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision and preimage attacks. Unsalted MD5 hashes can be trivially cracked using rainbow tables.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: The /register/customer endpoint stores customer passwords in plaintext (directly from request.json['password'] into the Customer model without any hashing). User passwords are never hashed, so a database breach would expose all customer passwords in cleartext.

cwe: CWE-312: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information

extra.message: The /fetch/customer endpoint retrieves customer records using a user-supplied 'id' value but performs no authorization check to verify the authenticated user is allowed to access that customer's record. Any authenticated user can access any customer's full PII including credit card numbers. This is an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR).

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The /get/<cust_id> endpoint retrieves customer records by URL path parameter but performs no authorization check to verify the authenticated user is allowed to access that customer's record. This endpoint also uses insecure_verify() which disables JWT signature verification (CWE-287), compounding the access control failure. Any caller can access any customer's full PII including credit card numbers.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Multiple endpoints return raw exception messages to the client via jsonify({'Error': str(e.message)}). This exposes internal application details (stack traces, database schema, library internals) to attackers, aiding in reconnaissance and vulnerability discovery. Affected endpoints include /register/user (line 148) and /register/customer (line 167).

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
